overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a highly effective academic and professional presence across decentralized networks (Bluesky, Mastodon) and traditional professional channels (LinkedIn). His content consistently addresses a niche, highly engaged audience interested in computational social science, disinformation, and academic recruitment. While LinkedIn provides the largest absolute reach and volume, Bluesky stands out with the highest relative engagement rates, reflecting a highly active and receptive network of peers.

contentThemes.description: Sharing scientific research, paper publications, and presentation announcements about coordinated online campaigns.
Promoting career milestones such as postdoc positions and PhD opportunities within computational social science.
Announcements of new software releases and algorithm improvements, specifically focusing on the CooRnet package.

recommendations: Capitalize on Bluesky's highly active ecosystem by utilizing it as the primary channel for raw research findings and interactive discussions.
Optimize LinkedIn for high-level professional achievements, funding announcements, and formal recruitment, which attract massive absolute engagement and comments.
Boost Mastodon engagement by initiating threads that break down complex methodological concepts, leveraging the academic focus of the AoIR instance.

platformInsights.bluesky: Secures the highest relative engagement rate (average 6.14%) across the profile, with users actively sharing and reposting academic opportunities and newly published research.
platformInsights.mastodon: Hosts a dedicated but smaller academic cohort (average engagement rate of 4.55%), showing strong interest in open-source tool updates like CooRnet.
platformInsights.linkedin: Acts as the largest amplifier (1,850 followers) with an average engagement rate of 4.87%, driving high comment volume and major visibility for institutional announcements.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
